skill.name: stripe-automation
skill.description: Automates Stripe customer, payment, subscription, invoice, product, price, and refund workflows through the Rube MCP Stripe toolkit.
skill.summary: Run guided Stripe operations through Rube MCP with current tool schemas and Stripe object guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The 64 external-command findings and the high weak-crypto and reconnaissance blockers are false positives from Markdown inline code and Stripe documentation text. The remaining risk is medium because the skill depends on a third-party MCP endpoint and documents state-changing Stripe billing operations.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Handles Stripe Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill requires Rube MCP and directs users to add the rube.app MCP endpoint for Stripe automation. This creates a third-party dependency for payment workflows and connected Stripe access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The MCP requirement and hardcoded Rube endpoint are explicit. The risk depends on user authorization and Rube account configuration, so it is not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: State-Changing Payment Workflows
medium_findings.description: The skill documents creation and modification of customers, payment intents, charges, subscriptions, invoices, and refunds. These actions can affect money movement or customer billing if used without confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed tool sequences include multiple write operations for payments and billing objects. The operations are legitimate for a Stripe skill, but they are high-impact.

medium_findings.title: Limited Built-In Guardrails for Live Billing Changes
medium_findings.description: The skill emphasizes connection setup and current schemas, but it does not require test mode, scoped permissions, or explicit approval before charge, subscription, invoice, or refund actions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced sections describe setup and write workflows without approval safeguards. This is a contextual risk from omissions, not a direct exploit.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Matches Are Markdown Tool Names
low_findings.description: The analyzer interpreted Markdown inline code around Rube and Stripe tool names as Ruby shell backticks. No executable Ruby, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is Markdown documentation using inline code formatting. There is no source file containing executable backtick command evaluation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography findings are false positives in prose and parameter documentation. No cryptographic algorithm implementation or weak hashing call was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain YAML description text, field names, and Stripe parameter guidance. No executable crypto code is present in the skill.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Matches Are Stripe Guidance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are false positives from normal Stripe workflow guidance, such as searching customers and using valid subscription inputs. No host or environment probing was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss Stripe records and billing requirements. They do not instruct the model to inspect the local machine or enumerate system data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded External MCP Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs users to add a fixed third-party MCP endpoint for Stripe operations. Users should verify the endpoint and account permissions before connecting live Stripe data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external URL is explicit and network use is required by the skill design. It is not malicious by itself, but it expands the trust boundary.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Stripe Write Actions
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow references create, update, capture, confirm, cancel, invoice, and refund actions. These are expected capabilities, but mistakes can change real customer billing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced workflow lines list state-changing Stripe tools directly. The concern is operational impact, not hidden behavior.

content.value_statement: Stripe billing work can be repetitive and error-prone across customers, invoices, subscriptions, and refunds. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for Rube MCP and active Stripe toolkit connections.
Lists Stripe customer workflows for search, creation, updates, and duplicate avoidance.
Documents payment intent, charge, and capture workflows with key parameters.
Covers subscription creation, retrieval, update, and cancellation workflows.
Explains invoice, product, price, and refund operations with Stripe object IDs.
Provides amount formatting, pagination, and Stripe ID prefix guidance.
limitations: Requires a working Rube MCP connection and authorized Stripe account access.
Does not include executable code, tests, or local validation scripts.
Does not enforce test mode, approval checks, or Stripe permission scoping.
Depends on live tool schemas returned by Rube MCP at runtime.

use_cases.title: Customer Support Billing Tasks
use_cases.description: Search customers, review charges, issue refunds, and check invoices with consistent Stripe object handling.

use_cases.title: Subscription Operations
use_cases.description: Create, inspect, and update customer subscriptions while using price IDs and current tool schemas.

use_cases.title: Payment Workflow Prototyping
use_cases.description: Draft payment intent, invoice, product, and refund workflows before implementing them in application code.

prompt_templates.title: Check Stripe Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Stripe automation skill to verify that Rube MCP is available and the Stripe toolkit connection is active. Do not change Stripe data.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Customer
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Stripe for a customer by this email: [email]. Show matching customer IDs, names, and duplicate risks before suggesting any update.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Subscription Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a subscription update for customer [customer_id] using price [price_id]. Confirm current state, amount units, and required approval before changes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Refund Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review charge [charge_id] for a potential partial refund of [amount]. Verify amount units, reason, customer impact, and confirmation steps.

output_examples.input: Find the Stripe customer for pat@example.com and check recent invoices.
output_examples.output: A concise customer summary with matching records, duplicate warnings, recent invoice statuses, and recommended next steps before any update.

output_examples.input: Prepare a refund for charge ch_123 for five dollars.
output_examples.output: A refund review that converts the amount to cents, checks the charge reference, notes customer impact, and asks for approval.

output_examples.input: Create a subscription for a customer using price price_123.
output_examples.output: A preflight checklist with customer, payment method, price, quantity, billing behavior, and approval status before creating the subscription.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so parameters match the current Stripe schema.
Use Stripe test mode or restricted permissions before live billing changes.
Require explicit user approval for charges, captures, invoices, subscriptions, and refunds.
anti_patterns: Do not run write operations after only matching a customer email.
Do not assume dollar amounts are Stripe amount values without conversion.
Do not connect live Stripe accounts to unverified MCP endpoints or broad permissions.

faq.question: Does this skill require Stripe API keys?
faq.answer: It uses Rube MCP connection management instead of asking the model to handle raw Stripe API keys.

faq.question: Can it create charges and refunds?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflows include payment intents, charges, captures, and refunds through Stripe tools.

faq.question: Is it safe for live Stripe accounts?
faq.answer: Use caution. Live accounts need restricted permissions, current schemas, and explicit approval before billing changes.

faq.question: Why should tools be searched first?
faq.answer: Rube tool schemas can change. Searching first reduces parameter errors and outdated workflow assumptions.

faq.question: Does it prevent duplicate customers?
faq.answer: It advises searching customers first because Stripe can allow multiple customers with the same email.

faq.question: Does it validate amounts automatically?
faq.answer: No. It explains smallest-unit formatting, but users should still verify amounts before payment actions.
